metadata.title: Capital → Compute → Capability (R3)
metadata.description: Better models win market share, share unlocks capital, capital buys compute, compute lifts capability. The loop where strategic investment (e.g. Google's $40B in Anthropic) plugs in.
metadata.author: Tracking AI Course

nodes.label: Model Capability
nodes.description: Aggregate quality of the frontier model.

nodes.label: Market Share
nodes.description: Share of paid frontier-model usage and enterprise contracts.

nodes.label: Capital
nodes.description: Cash from revenue plus strategic investment available to spend on training. Includes deals like Google's $40B commitment to Anthropic.

nodes.label: Compute Scale
nodes.description: Total training compute available — GPUs, datacenters, electricity contracts.

nodes.label: Training Throughput
nodes.description: How quickly the lab can train new frontier models.

edges.description: Market share converts to revenue and unlocks strategic investment.
Capital buys GPUs, power, and datacenters.
More compute means faster, larger training runs.

delay.description: Compute investments take quarters to translate into deployed capability.

loops.label: R3: Capital → Compute → Capability
loops.description: A persistent capital advantage weakens compute scarcity for the leader specifically, while it keeps biting everyone else.
loops.behavior_pattern: Asymmetric scaling — the rich get richer in compute access.
